::: {.panel-tabset}

## Intercept-Only 

```{.tikz}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=2, font=\Large]
  % Equation with smaller gaps
  \node (y) at (0,0) {$\mathbf{y}$};
  \node (eq) at (0.8,0) {$=$};
  \node (mu) at (1.6,0) {$\mu$};
  \node (plus) at (2.4,0) {$+$};
  \node (e) at (3.2,0) {$\mathbf{e}$};
  
  % Arrows pointing down
  \draw[->, thick, black] (mu) -- (1.6, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 50)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (e) -- (3.2, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_R \mathbf{I})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (3.4, -1) -- (3.4, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

## Linear Regression

```{.tikz}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=2, font=\Large]
  % Equation with smaller gaps
  \node (y) at (0,0) {$\mathbf{y}$};
  \node (eq) at (0.8,0) {$=$};
  \node (mu) at (1.6,0) {$\mu$};
  \node (plus) at (2.4,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_b) at (3.2,0) {$\beta_B\bar{\mathbf{x}}$};
  \node (plus) at (4.0,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_w) at (4.8,0) {$\beta_W(\mathbf{x}-\bar{\mathbf{x}})$};
  \node (plus) at (5.6,0) {$+$};
  \node (e) at (6.4,0) {$\mathbf{e}$};
  
  % Arrows pointing down
  \draw[->, thick, black] (mu) -- (1.6, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 50)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (3.1, -0.15) -- (3.1, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (4.4, -0.15) -- (4.4, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (e) -- (6.4, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_R \mathbf{I})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (6.6, -1) -- (6.6, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

## MultiPhylo

```{.tikz}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=2, font=\Large]
  % Equation with smaller gaps
  \node (y) at (0,0) {$\mathbf{y}$};
  \node (eq) at (0.8,0) {$=$};
  \node (mu) at (1.6,0) {$\mu$};
  \node (plus) at (2.4,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_b) at (3.2,0) {$\beta_B\bar{\mathbf{x}}$};
  \node (plus) at (4.0,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_w) at (4.8,0) {$\beta_W(\mathbf{x}-\bar{\mathbf{x}})$};
  \node (plus) at (5.6,0) {$+$};
  \node (a) at (6.4,0) {$\mathbf{a}$};
  \node (plus) at (7.2,0) {$+$};
  \node (s) at (8.0,0) {$\mathbf{s}$};
  \node (plus) at (8.8,0) {$+$};
  \node (e) at (9.6,0) {$\mathbf{e}$};
  
  % Arrows pointing down
  \draw[->, thick, black] (mu) -- (1.6, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 50)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (3.1, -0.15) -- (3.1, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (4.4, -0.15) -- (4.4, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (a) -- (6.4, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_P \mathbf{L})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (6.6, -1) -- (6.6, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (s) -- (8.0, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_S \mathbf{I})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (8.2, -1) -- (8.2, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (e) -- (9.6, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_R \mathbf{I})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (9.8, -1) -- (9.8, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

## BYM2Params

```{.tikz}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=2, font=\Large]
  % Equation with smaller gaps
  \node (y) at (0,0) {$\mathbf{y}$};
  \node (eq) at (0.8,0) {$=$};
  \node (mu) at (1.6,0) {$\mu$};
  \node (plus) at (2.4,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_b) at (3.2,0) {$\beta_B\bar{\mathbf{x}}$};
  \node (plus) at (4.0,0) {$+$};
  \node (beta_w) at (4.8,0) {$\beta_W(\mathbf{x}-\bar{\mathbf{x}})$};
  \node (plus) at (5.6,0) {$+$};
  \node (bym) at (7.2,0) {$\sigma_T (\sqrt{1-\rho}\ \mathbf{a}^* + \sqrt{\rho}\ \mathbf{s}^*)$};
  \node (plus) at (8.8,0) {$+$};
  \node (e) at (9.6,0) {$\mathbf{e}$};
  
  % Arrows pointing down
  \draw[->, thick, black] (mu) -- (1.6, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 50)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (3.1, -0.15) -- (3.1, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (4.4, -0.15) -- (4.4, -1.5) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(0, 10)$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (6.1, -0.15) -- (6.1, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
  
  \draw[thick, black] (7.05, -0.15) -- (7.05, -0.45);
  \draw[thick, black] (7.05, -0.45) -- (6.5, -0.45);
  \draw[thick, black] (6.5, -0.45) -- (6.5, -1.05);
  \draw[thick, black] (6.5, -1.05) -- (7.4, -1.05);
  \draw[->, thick, black] (7.4, -1.05) -- (7.4, -1.5) node[below] {$Beta(0.5, 0.5)$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (7.25, -0.15) -- (7.25, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \mathbf{L})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (8.2, -0.15) -- (8.2, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \mathbf{I})$};
  
  \draw[->, thick, black] (e) -- (9.6, -0.6) node[below] {$\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0}, \sigma_R \mathbf{I})$};
  \draw[->, thick, black] (9.8, -1) -- (9.8, -1.5) node[below] {$t_3(0,20)$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

Past Questions:

- Do we need the scaling factor here ($\mathbf{a}^*$ and $\mathbf{s}^*$)? cause the variance is already 1, no? For now, i will skip the scaling process
  - reference: In order for $\sigma$ to legitimately be the standard deviation of the random effect, it is critical that, for each $i$, $Var(\theta_i)\approx Var(\phi_i)\approx 1$. This is not automatic for ICAR models, where every component of $\theta$ will have a different variance
  - answer: no, it's not needed in our case
- Is the sum-to-zero constraint needed? ```sum(phy_eff) ~ normal(0, 0.001 * J)```
  - answer: no

:::
